annotations.label: Martingale (informal)

anchor.snippet: a martingale is a stochastic process in which the expected value of the next observation

annotations.note: The Mathlib definition `Martingale f ℱ μ := StronglyAdapted ℱ f ∧ ∀ i ≤ j, μ[f j | ℱ i] =ᵐ[μ] f i` directly captures the informal idea that the conditional expectation of the next value equals the present value.

annotations.label: Discrete-time martingale

anchor.snippet: A basic definition of a discrete-time martingale is a discrete-time stochastic process

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Martingale` is defined for any `Preorder ι`, specializing to the discrete-time case when `ι = ℕ`.

annotations.label: Martingale with respect to another sequence

anchor.section: Martingale sequences with respect to another sequence
anchor.snippet: is said to be a martingale with respect to another sequence

annotations.note: Mathlib defines martingales relative to a filtration rather than another process directly; the notion is recoverable by combining `Martingale` with `Filtration.natural` of the reference process, but is not packaged as a single decl.

annotations.label: Continuous-time martingale with respect to a process

anchor.section: Martingale sequences with respect to another sequence
anchor.snippet: Similarly, a continuous-time martingale with respect to the stochastic process

annotations.note: Same as the discrete case: `Martingale` over a continuous index `ι` together with `Filtration.natural` of the conditioning process expresses this, but it is not a dedicated decl.

annotations.label: General martingale (Banach-space valued)

anchor.snippet: In full generality, a stochastic process

annotations.note: `Martingale` is defined with values in any `E` that is a `NormedAddCommGroup`/`NormedSpace ℝ E`/`CompleteSpace`, i.e. Banach-space valued, on any `Preorder ι`.

annotations.label: Unbiased random walk

anchor.snippet: An unbiased random walk , in any number of dimensions, is an example of a martingale

annotations.note: No declaration constructing the unbiased random walk and proving it is a martingale was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Gambler's fortune in a fair game

anchor.snippet: A gambler's fortune (capital) is a martingale if all the betting games which the gambler plays are fair

annotations.note: No Mathlib decl formalizes the gambler-fortune fair-game example as a martingale.

annotations.label: Squared-fortune martingale

annotations.note: The martingale `X_n^2 − n` for a fair-coin walk is not formalized as a named decl.

annotations.note: No Mathlib declaration was found for de Moivre's martingale.

annotations.note: Pólya's urn process is not formalized in `Mathlib/Probability`.

annotations.note: No Mathlib decl establishes the sequence of likelihood ratios as a martingale.

annotations.label: Doob martingale

anchor.snippet: Doob martingale : Let

annotations.note: `martingale_condExp` proves that `fun i => μ[f | ℱ i]` is a martingale, which is precisely the Doob martingale construction.

annotations.note: This ecological application is not formalized.

annotations.label: Allele frequency under genetic drift

anchor.snippet: In population genetics , the frequency of an allele in a population of fixed size

annotations.note: Wright–Fisher / genetic-drift allele-frequency martingale is not formalized.

annotations.note: No Poisson-process construction or compensated-Poisson martingale was found in Mathlib.

anchor.snippet: Exponential martingale : If

annotations.note: Brownian motion and its exponential martingale are not formalized in Mathlib.

anchor.snippet: Wald's martingale : If

anchor.snippet: dimensional process

annotations.note: `Martingale` is parameterized by an arbitrary Banach space `E`, so the multi-dimensional case `E = ℝ^d` is a specialization.

annotations.note: `Submartingale f ℱ μ` is defined for any `Preorder ι`, specializing to `ι = ℕ` for the discrete-time case.

anchor.snippet: Likewise, a continuous-time submartingale satisfies

annotations.note: Same `Submartingale` decl handles continuous time when `ι` is e.g. `ℝ≥0` or `ℝ`.

annotations.label: Discrete-time supermartingale

anchor.section: Submartingales, supermartingales, and relationship to harmonic functions
anchor.snippet: Supermartingales: Analogously, a discrete-time supermartingale satisfies

annotations.note: `Supermartingale f ℱ μ` is defined for any `Preorder ι`; the discrete case is `ι = ℕ`.

annotations.label: Continuous-time supermartingale

anchor.section: Submartingales, supermartingales, and relationship to harmonic functions
anchor.snippet: Likewise, a continuous-time supermartingale satisfies

annotations.note: `Supermartingale` is index-agnostic and covers continuous time.

annotations.label: Martingale iff sub- and supermartingale

anchor.snippet: Every martingale is also a submartingale and a supermartingale

annotations.note: `martingale_iff` states `Martingale f ℱ μ ↔ Supermartingale f ℱ μ ∧ Submartingale f ℱ μ`.

annotations.label: Biased coin gambler — martingale case

anchor.snippet: the gambler on average neither wins nor loses money, and the fortune over time is a martingale

annotations.note: Not formalized as a named example in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Biased coin gambler — supermartingale case

anchor.snippet: the gambler loses money on average, and the fortune over time is a supermartingale

annotations.note: Not formalized as a named example in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Biased coin gambler — submartingale case

anchor.snippet: the gambler wins money on average, and the fortune over time is a submartingale

annotations.note: Not formalized as a named example in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Convex function of a martingale is a submartingale

anchor.snippet: A convex function of a martingale is a submartingale, by Jensen's inequality

annotations.note: No `ConvexOn`-of-martingale-is-submartingale decl was located in `Mathlib/Probability`.

annotations.label: Stopping time

anchor.snippet: A stopping time with respect to a sequence of random variables

annotations.note: `IsStoppingTime f τ` is defined as the condition that `{ω | τ ω ≤ i}` is `f i`-measurable for every `i`.

annotations.label: Stopped martingale is a martingale

anchor.snippet: One of the basic properties of martingales is that

annotations.note: Only `Submartingale.stoppedProcess` is provided; the martingale (and supermartingale) versions are not stated as their own decls, though they follow via `martingale_iff` and negation.

annotations.label: Optional stopping theorem

anchor.snippet: the optional stopping theorem which states that, under certain conditions

annotations.note: This iff-form of Doob's optional stopping theorem characterizes submartingales via monotonicity of expected stopped values over bounded stopping times.

annotations.note: The Stroock–Varadhan martingale problem formulation is not formalized in Mathlib.
